name: kaggle-competition-coordinator
title: Kaggle Competition Coordinator
description: Coordinates a team through a Kaggle competition: gathers per-member profiles via fan-out, matches competitions to the team, gates on a competition lead's approval, creates a repo, runs a reviewer approve/revise loop on the proposed solution, submits to Kaggle, and returns to the lead with the score to decide next steps.
preamble:
## Domain Context
A team completes Kaggle competitions as a coordinated effort. The competition lead approves which competition to enter and decides next steps after each submission. Each team member self-reports strengths, weaknesses, interests, and current load; those profiles drive both competition matching and the automatic selection of a best-fit reviewer (not necessarily the lead) who runs an approve/revise loop on the proposed solution. Kaggle access (list, submit, score) is authenticated via the Kaggle CLI; the submittable file is produced by the team and uploaded before submission.

triggerInput.name: namespace
triggerInput.description: Namespace handle to fetch candidate team members from

triggerInput.name: profileDurationHours
triggerInput.description: Hours to wait for team members to submit their profiles (e.g. 0.5 = 30min)

triggerInput.name: githubOwner
triggerInput.description: GitHub org or user under which the competition repository is created

triggerInput.name: competitionHint
triggerInput.description: Optional search term to focus the Kaggle competition listing (e.g. 'tabular', 'nlp')

steps.name: Fetch members
steps.description: Fetch namespace members from the platform API as table-editor options.

steps.name: Select team members
steps.description: Competition lead selects which members take part in this competition.

config.emptyMessage: No members found in the namespace.

steps.name: Prepare team
steps.description: Filter the included members and compute the profile collection deadline.

steps.name: Gather profiles
steps.description: Spawn a gather-profile child workflow for each selected team member.

steps.name: Wait for profiles
steps.description: Wait until the profile collection deadline passes.

steps.name: Collect profiles
steps.description: Fetch each spawned child instance and extract the member profile.

steps.name: Fetch competitions
steps.description: List open Kaggle competitions via the Kaggle CLI, optionally filtered by competitionHint.

steps.name: Match competitions
steps.description: Rank the fetched competitions against the aggregated team profile and produce a selectable shortlist.

steps.name: Approve competition
steps.description: Competition lead picks one competition from the shortlist and approves entering it, or rejects to search again.

steps.name: Create repository
steps.description: Create a private GitHub repository for the approved competition via the GitHub API.

steps.name: Select reviewer
steps.description: Pick the best-fit reviewer from the team based on expertise, interests, and current load.

agent.prompt:
# Select the Solution Reviewer

Read `/output/input.json`:
- `steps.collect_profiles.profiles[]` — `{ userId, email, strengths, weaknesses, interests, currentLoad }`.
- `steps.review_competitions` — the approved competition `{ competitionRef, title, category, ... }`.

## Task
Choose exactly one team member to review the proposed solution. Favour relevant expertise and interest in the competition's domain; penalise high `currentLoad`. The reviewer need not be the competition lead. Pick a `userId` that exists in `profiles`.

## Output contract (MANDATORY)
1. Write the result JSON to `/output/result.json` using bash.
2. Your FINAL message must be ONLY: {"output_file": "/output/result.json", "summary": "..."}

Result JSON shape:
```json
{ "reviewerId": "<userId from profiles>", "reviewerEmail": "<email>", "rationale": "<why this member, referencing expertise and load>" }
```

steps.name: Propose solution
steps.description: Draft a solution proposal for the competition. On revise loops, incorporate the reviewer's feedback.

agent.prompt:
# Propose a Solution

Read `/output/input.json`:
- `steps.review_competitions` — the approved competition `{ competitionRef, title, category, deadline }`.
- `steps.collect_profiles.profiles[]` — the team's strengths and interests.
- `steps.create_repo` — `{ repoUrl, repoName }` for the competition repository.
- `steps.review_proposal` (only present on revise loops) — the reviewer's `feedback` to address.

## Task
Produce a concrete solution proposal the team can execute: problem framing, data understanding, modelling approach, validation strategy, a task breakdown mapped to team members' strengths, and risks. If reviewer feedback is present, revise the proposal to address it explicitly.

## Output contract (MANDATORY)
1. Write the result JSON to `/output/result.json` using bash.
2. Your FINAL message must be ONLY: {"output_file": "/output/result.json", "summary": "..."}

Result JSON shape:
```json
{ "proposal": "<full proposal in markdown>", "summary": "<2-3 sentence summary of the approach and what changed since last round>" }
```

steps.name: Review proposal
steps.description: The selected reviewer approves the proposal for submission, or revises with feedback to send it back.

params.name: feedback
params.description: What to change before this can be submitted (required when revising)

steps.name: Provide submission file
steps.description: Upload the final submission file the team produced for the approved solution.

steps.name: Submit to Kaggle
steps.description: Submit the uploaded file to Kaggle and poll the submissions endpoint until the validation score lands.

steps.name: Decide next steps
steps.description: Review the Kaggle validation feedback and decide what the team does next.
